checks.look: `stream=` usage and whether the stream is joined before returning, back-to-back awaits, per-row LLM loops, one-at-a-time embedding calls.
checks.fail: no streaming on a user-facing chat path, or a sync client blocking an async handler: Medium.

checks.title: An eval harness gates change: a golden dataset with negative, adversarial, and refusal cases per quality-critical feature, scoring fit to the output type, a CI gate with a committed baseline blocking prompt, few-shot, and model-id changes, cassettes instead of live calls, temperature 0 on asserted tests
checks.look: `evals/` and test dirs, CI workflows that invoke them, baseline files, provider hits in CI.
checks.fail: no eval for a quality-critical feature, or a committed suite CI never runs: High.

checks.title: Judges and specialized evals are sound: rubric, randomized pairwise order, and a different model family for any LLM judge; agent evals assert trajectory (tools, order, termination); RAG gets a retrieval eval (recall@k, precision@k, MRR, or nDCG) plus a grounding check
checks.look: judge prompts and model config inside eval code, trajectory assertions, labeled query-doc sets.
checks.fail: a same-family judge with fixed order: Medium; a RAG feature with only end-to-end eval: Medium.

checks.title: Observability is attached: a tracing wrapper on every call capturing prompt, response, model, latency, outcome, and usage; a shared trace id threaded through agent and RAG child steps; p95 and p99 latency plus typed error metrics with alerts; a feedback signal joinable to traces
checks.look: wrapper or decorator coverage versus raw call sites, trace-id threading, metric and alert config.
checks.fail: raw SDK calls bypassing the tracer, or instrumentation constructed but never attached: Medium. Log redaction gaps file as F-SEC; fleet alerting shape as F-OBS, per the ownership map.

checks.title: Embedding parity and lifecycle: one pinned model and dimension across ingestion and query with the version stored in the index, the index metric matching the embedding, a reindex path triggered by edits, deletes, and model bumps, structure-aware chunking with overlap under the token limit, provenance carried into the prompt
checks.look: embedding constants read by both paths, index config, reindex scripts and their deploy or cron wiring, the chunker.
checks.fail: ingestion and query embedding with different models or dimensions (retrieval silently near-random): Critical; no reindex path: High.

checks.title: Retrieval is tuned: hybrid dense plus BM25 with rank fusion where the corpus holds exact identifiers, reranker output actually ordering results, top-k with a relevance threshold and an explicit empty-result branch, ANN parameters sized to the corpus, asymmetric prefixes where the model requires them, per-tenant ACL filters at query time
checks.look: retriever query builders, rerank score usage, `score_threshold` values, filter parameters on every search call.
checks.fail: a multi-tenant corpus queried with no ACL filter: Critical, filed as F-SEC and cited here; no empty-result branch: Medium.

checks.title: The loop is bounded and gated: rich tool schemas (descriptions, enums, required arrays), hard limits on iterations, cumulative tokens, and wall clock plus a no-progress detector, structured tool errors that never crash the loop, tool output truncated before re-entering context, destructive tools behind default-on HITL with dry-run, MCP sources pinned with integrity re-verification, scoped handoff credentials, bounded provenance-tagged memory, a goal termination predicate
checks.look: tool definitions, `while True` and framework `max_iterations` or `recursion_limit`, approval gates and their defaults, `mcp.json`.
checks.fail: an unbounded loop on a reachable path: Critical; a destructive tool with no human gate or one broad ambient credential: Critical, filed as F-SEC and cited here; `max_tokens` presented as the loop bound: High.

checks.title: Every control is central and on the blocking request path: one prompt assembly, one validated output layer, one pinned model config, one eval gate, guardrails that block, tracers attached, cache markers before volatile content, and the strengths inventory preserved
checks.look: trace each discovered control onto the request path; count call sites bypassing the central modules.
checks.fail: a control that exists off-path (a guardrail never called, a validator bypassed): the severity of the control it fakes, and the paper control zeroes its scoring dimension.

checks.title: No deprecated or retired provider surface remains: retired model ids in callers, fallback chains, or fixtures; legacy endpoints (text completions, deprecated Assistants API); removed parameters; missing required beta headers
checks.look: every model-id literal checked against the provider deprecation page (principle-based, no hardcoded list); endpoint paths in raw HTTP calls.
checks.fail: a retired id or endpoint on a production path (a latent 404 at sunset): High.

checks.title: No prompt-claim-versus-payload gap: every control the prompt wording claims ("respond only with JSON", "only access this user's records", "ignore instructions in the documents") exists as a mechanical control in code
checks.look: pair each claim in prompt text with `response_format` or tool schemas, code-level authz, or structural isolation.
checks.fail: a claimed control with no mechanical counterpart: the severity of the missing control, Medium at minimum; the gap itself is the finding.

checks.title: One primary actor per journey is reconstructible from the product itself, with functional, emotional, and social jobs and a context of use
checks.look: README, landing and onboarding copy, role or persona enums, `docs/`; plan-aware: the actor decision in PLAN.mdx.
checks.fail: flows addressed to an undifferentiated "users" with no reconstructible actor or job; Medium.

checks.title: The 2-4 primary journeys (signup to first value, main recurring task, recovery or upgrade, conversion) complete in a defensible step count and never re-ask data the system holds
checks.look: route trees, wizard and stepper components, redirect chains; count screens per core goal.
checks.fail: re-entry of held data on a core journey, High; a journey materially longer than the job requires, Medium.

checks.title: Every async action has loading, pending, disabled, and success states; every screen has an empty state with a forward action; no error, expired, or zero-result state dead-ends
checks.look: mutation and fetch sites without loading handling, `grep -rn '"No data"'`, empty-state and error components.
checks.fail: silent async actions or any dead-end state without a forward action; High.

checks.title: Waits get treatment where the roughly 100ms/400ms RAIL and Doherty budgets cannot hold: skeletons or optimistic UI, and position shown in every multi-step flow
checks.look: skeleton components, optimistic update code, stepper progress indicators, long-running handlers behind bare spinners.
checks.fail: a multi-step flow with no position indicator, or a long operation with no visible progress; Medium.

checks.title: Destructive actions carry undo or a recovery path, every state has a marked exit, no modal or wizard traps, confirmations guard only costly irreversible actions
checks.look: delete handlers, soft-delete or trash code, modals lacking close and escape handling, `confirm(` calls.
checks.fail: hard delete with neither undo nor confirmation, High; confirmations on trivial actions, Low.

checks.title: One term per concept and one label per action product-wide; buttons name the action or outcome, never "Submit", "OK", or "Click here"
checks.look: i18n catalogs and button strings; grep pairs like "Sign in" versus "Log in" for the same concept.
checks.fail: the same concept under two names, or generic labels on primary actions; Medium.

checks.title: Errors state what happened, why, how to fix it, and what happens next, at the field, in plain language; raw codes and stack traces never render; user input survives the error
checks.look: catch blocks rendering `err.message` or `error.code`, error catalogs, form reset calls inside error paths.
checks.fail: raw codes or stack traces reaching users, or a form cleared on error; High.

checks.title: Flow accessibility holds against the stated or inferable conformance target: full keyboard operability with no traps, focus order following visual order, no redundant entry of provided data, paste and autofill never blocked in auth. Contrast tokens and focus visuals are ui's: cross-reference F-UI per the ownership map
checks.look: custom widgets without key handlers, paste handlers calling `preventDefault`, `autocomplete="off"` on credential fields, `tabIndex` abuse.
checks.fail: a keyboard-unreachable core task, Critical; blocked paste or autofill in auth, High.

checks.title: Forms meet the Baymard standard: persistent visible labels, inline validation on blur, minimal field count, correct `type` and `inputmode`, standard `autocomplete` tokens, format-tolerant normalization, double-submit guards, never clearing on error
checks.look: `grep -rln "placeholder=" src | xargs grep -L "<label"`, validation triggers, submit handlers.
checks.fail: placeholder-as-label or missing autocomplete on identity fields, one systemic finding; High.

checks.title: Every multi-actor process runs on an explicit state machine: start and end states, a join for every branch, exception paths, no unreachable states
checks.look: state-machine or workflow files, xstate or step configs, status enums whose transitions scatter across handlers.
checks.fail: stringly-typed status mutated ad hoc from many call sites with no machine; High.

checks.title: Workflow integrity mechanics exist: server-side role checks on every transition, timeout or escalation for stalled items, optimistic locking or a conflict path, status visibility for waiting parties, and in-product reassign, recall, and rollback
checks.look: transition endpoints and their middleware, escalation jobs, `version` checks, reassignment routes.
checks.fail: any of the five missing on a live workflow, High; an exploitable authorization hole cross-references F-SEC instead of double-billing.

checks.title: Flows carry no unexamined waste: no serial approvals that could parallelize, no deterministic rule-based human steps a machine should do, a locatable bottleneck. Plan-aware: the TIMWOODS classification table exists
checks.look: approval chains in workflow code, manual routing or status-setting endpoints, the longest serial screen chain.
checks.fail: serial approvals with independent approvers, or automatable human steps; Medium, Tentative without runtime data.

checks.title: Navigation matches the actor's mental model: labels with information scent in user vocabulary, breadcrumbs on deep pages, a current-location indicator, and typo-tolerant search with a useful zero-results state where volume warrants
checks.look: nav config, sidebar and menu components, breadcrumb and search implementations; trace two realistic find-X tasks.
checks.fail: nav mirroring the code's module names or the org chart, or no current-location signal; Medium.

checks.title: The activation event is identifiable, the signup-to-value path carries no unjustified walls (account, email verification, card, sales call), and first-run drives the core action with templates or sample data, not a tour or a blank canvas
checks.look: onboarding routes, verification gates, tour libraries in `package.json`, seed data.
checks.fail: an unjustified wall before first value, High; feature-tour or blank-canvas first-run, Medium.

checks.title: Repeat-use products have a retention loop: saved state worth returning to and value-driven re-engagement, not spam
checks.look: persistence of drafts and history, notification and digest jobs, re-engagement email triggers.
checks.fail: re-engagement sends with no user value, or no saved state behind the return trigger; Low.

checks.title: Responsiveness basics hold: a loading state on every async action, viewport meta with zoom enabled, no horizontal scroll at 320px, thumb-sized touch targets. CWV code signals (unsized media, render blocking) are seo's: cross-reference F-SEO
checks.look: `grep -rn "user-scalable=no"`, viewport meta tags, target sizing in component styles.
checks.fail: `user-scalable=no` anywhere, High; a numeric CWV verdict asserted from static code is refused: mark Tentative with the confirming Lighthouse command named.

checks.title: No deceptive patterns: cancellation as easy as signup, "Reject all" as prominent and as few clicks as "Accept all", total pricing before commitment, no pre-checked opt-ins, no fake scarcity or manufactured social proof, granular revocable consent
checks.look: billing and subscription routes, consent banners, `defaultChecked`, countdown timers, hardcoded viewer or stock counts.
checks.fail: any confirmed deceptive pattern, Critical; legal exposure under GDPR, the FTC click-to-cancel rule, and EU DSA Art. 25.
checks.dimension: Trust and anti-deception (conditional: billing or consent)

checks.title: Interactive components carry hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, empty, and error states; interactive elements look interactive; one dominant primary action per screen; expert accelerators never block novices
checks.look: design-system component prop surfaces, `div` elements with `onClick` and no role, screens with competing primary buttons.
checks.fail: custom controls missing disabled and loading states, or clickable divs masquerading as buttons; Medium. Focus-visible styling itself is visual a11y: cross-reference F-UI per the ownership map.

checks.title: Context survives cross-channel and cross-device handoffs, multi-step flows show remaining progress, and each journey ends on a designed note rather than a dead stop
checks.look: email link targets and their post-auth redirects, resume tokens, deep-link handling, success screens.
checks.fail: a handoff that drops the user at a bare login and loses their place, High; anticlimactic endings, Low.

checks.title: Non-GUI surfaces get the same discipline: exit codes and messages follow the error standard, help output teaches the next step, destructive commands take confirmation or `--dry-run`, API errors are specific and recovery-oriented
checks.look: CLI entry and argument parser, destructive subcommands, API error payload shapes.
checks.fail: a destructive command with no guard, High; bare 500s or opaque API error bodies, Medium.

checks.title: No UX theater: spinners bound to no request, progress bars reflecting nothing, confirmations guarding nothing costly, accessibility labels describing the wrong thing
checks.look: read the binding behind each state widget: which promise drives the spinner, which value feeds the bar.
checks.fail: any element performing UX without doing its job; High, because it actively misleads.

checks.title: Dates, numbers, and currency match the user's locale, and format hints appear where input format matters
checks.look: hardcoded date and currency formats, `Intl.` or locale-library usage versus string concatenation.
checks.fail: hardcoded single-locale formats in a product with multi-locale reach; Low.

checks.title: The stack contract is reconstructable and singular: one framework, one rendering model, one styling model, exactly one design-token source, a named design system, deliberate Web Components use
checks.look: `package.json`, `next.config.*`, `astro.config.*`, `nuxt.config.*`, `vite.config.*`, `tailwind.config.*`, `tokens.json`, `theme.ts`, `:root` blocks.
checks.fail: two competing token sources or styling models with neither authoritative: Medium. Plan-aware: code contradicting a PLAN.mdx stack decision: High.

checks.title: Native elements do interactive work: `button` for actions, `a[href]` for navigation, native inputs, `details`/`summary` disclosure, `dialog` modals
checks.look: `grep -rEn '<(div|span)[^>]*(onClick|@click|on:click)' src/` across `*.jsx`, `*.tsx`, `*.vue`, `*.svelte`, `*.html`.
checks.fail: a `div`/`span` doing a control's work without role, `tabindex="0"`, and a key handler: High; when it locks keyboard users out of a load-bearing control: Critical.

checks.title: Every control has an accessible name, every field a programmatic label, images follow an alt policy, load-bearing media has captions or a transcript
checks.look: icon-only buttons whose only child is an `svg`; `input`/`select`/`textarea` without `for`/`id`, wrapping `label`, or `aria-label`; missing `alt`; placeholder-only fields; `video`/`audio` sites.
checks.fail: unnamed core control or captionless load-bearing media: Critical; placeholder-only labels or informative `alt=""`: High.

checks.title: ARIA is valid and keyboard discipline holds: widget roles carry mandatory states, ID references resolve, no `aria-hidden` on focusables, no positive `tabindex`, a skip link first, APG keyboard models on custom widgets
checks.look: `grep -rEn 'role="(tab|checkbox|combobox|menu|listbox)"' src/` then read for `aria-selected`/`aria-checked` and arrow-key handlers; `grep -rEn 'tabindex="[1-9]' src/`.
checks.fail: a custom widget with no keyboard path on a load-bearing surface: Critical; positive `tabindex`, unresolved refs, or `aria-hidden` wrapping focusables: High.

checks.title: Overlays run through one shared focus-managed primitive: focus set on open, trapped while open, restored on close, Escape dismisses, portal-rendered, `showModal()` or `role="dialog"` plus `aria-modal`
checks.look: overlay sources in `src/components/ui/`; `grep -rn '\.show()' src/`; per-feature dialog copies.
checks.fail: a focus trap with no keyboard escape or a non-dismissable full-page overlay: Critical; `dialog.show()` for modal use: High; parallel overlay implementations: Medium.

checks.title: Focus stays visible: a `:focus-visible` baseline exists and survives the cascade; no `outline: none` without a replacement indicator
checks.look: `grep -rn 'outline' src/styles/` and the reset; rule order against every `:focus-visible` declaration.
checks.fail: a global reset strips outlines with no replacement, or the `:focus-visible` rule is defeated by a later selector: High.

checks.title: Motion is gated: animation behind `prefers-reduced-motion`, `transform`/`opacity` only, pause controls on auto-advancing content
checks.look: `grep -rn 'prefers-reduced-motion' src/`; `@keyframes` and `transition` sites; carousel, ticker, and auto-dismiss toast components.
checks.fail: auto-advancing content with no pause/stop/extend control: High; ungated animation or layout-property animation (`width`, `top`, `margin`): Medium.

checks.title: Status and shell basics are wired: errors via `aria-describedby` plus `aria-invalid`, live regions written by real code paths, semantic input types with `autocomplete` and `inputmode`, `html lang` set, zoom never blocked
checks.look: `grep -rEn 'user-scalable=no|maximum-scale=1' src/`; `aria-live`/`role="status"` regions and their writers; form error rendering; the document shell.
checks.fail: zoom disabled: Critical; a live region nothing writes to, or errors not tied to their fields: High; missing `lang`: Medium.

checks.title: Document structure holds per route: one `h1`, no skipped levels, a single `main` with landmarks, no stranded content, valid nesting, no duplicate ids, descriptive per-route titles, complete shell metadata
checks.look: route templates and layouts; `grep -rEn '<(main|h1)' src/`; interactive-in-interactive nesting (`button` inside `a`); duplicate id values.
checks.fail: nested interactive elements or duplicate ids breaking ARIA references: High; missing or multiple `h1`/`main`, skipped levels, generic titles: Medium.

checks.title: Styling architecture is deliberate: a cascade strategy, no `!important` wars, a z-index scale, logical properties for RTL safety, Flexbox/Grid primitives, print styles on report surfaces
checks.look: `grep -rc '!important' src/`; `grep -rEn 'z-index: *[0-9]{3,}' src/`; physical `margin-left` density vs `margin-inline`; `@media print` when invoice or report routes exist.
checks.fail: `!important` wars or magic z-index stacks: Medium; a report surface with no print styles, or RTL scope built on physical properties: Medium.

checks.title: Data views render the full state matrix: loading, empty, error, and success wired to real state; controlled inputs paired with `onChange`; stable keys; portal overlays; error boundaries on data trees
checks.look: fetching and mutating components; `grep -rEn 'key=\{(index|i)\}' src/`; `value=` without `onChange`; error boundary presence.
checks.fail: a load-bearing form frozen (controlled input, no `onChange`) or losing input on submit: Critical; happy-path-only data views: High; index keys on mutable lists: Medium.

checks.title: The responsive contract is real: reflow at 320 CSS px, fluid containers, dimensions reserved on media, targets at least 24x24 CSS px, no hover-only affordances, click alternatives to drag, `rem`/`em` text, safe-area insets
checks.look: fixed container widths (`grep -rEn 'width: *[0-9]{3,}px' src/`); `img`/`iframe` without `width`/`height`/`aspect-ratio`; `:hover`-only menus; breakpoints vs content min-widths.
checks.fail: fixed-width layout forcing horizontal scroll at 320px: Critical; hover-only navigation: High; undimensioned media: Medium.

checks.title: The render path is wired: the LCP element prioritized (`fetchpriority="high"` or the framework `priority` prop), never lazy above the fold, critical fonts preloaded with `font-display`, below-fold images lazy, long lists virtualized
checks.look: hero and landing components; positions of `grep -rn 'loading="lazy"' src/` hits; font preload in the shell; list renderers over large collections.
checks.fail: `loading="lazy"` on the LCP hero: High; unvirtualized thousand-row lists or fonts without `font-display`: Medium. Sitewide CWV code signals cross-reference F-SEO; numeric CWV claims stay Tentative.

checks.title: Token discipline holds: no raw hex/rgb or off-scale spacing, no arbitrary utility values, no hand-rolled duplicates of existing primitives, no inline restyling of DS components per use
checks.look: `grep -rEn '#[0-9a-fA-F]{3,6}' src/components/` outside the token source; `grep -rEn '\[[0-9]+px\]|\[#' src/` for arbitrary Tailwind values; near-duplicate primitives.
checks.fail: a decorative token source (declared, widely bypassed): High; scattered raw literals: Medium.

checks.title: Theming swaps end to end when in scope: every color routed through tokens that fully swap, `color-scheme` set, no subtree bypassing the provider, custom properties crossing any shadow boundary
checks.look: `data-theme` or `.dark` selectors against the token file; hardcoded colors inside themed components; `color-scheme` in globals; `:host` plumbing in Web Components.
checks.fail: a half-hardcoded theme (dark mode leaves literals unswapped): High; missing `color-scheme`: Low. Absent theming: record the absence and skip.

checks.title: The asset pipeline is sound: modern formats with `srcset`/`sizes`, per-icon imports or a sprite, optimized SVGs, subset preloaded fonts, a complete favicon/manifest set, iframes carrying `title`, `sandbox`, `loading="lazy"`
checks.look: `public/` and `static/` weights and formats; whole-library icon imports; SVG editor cruft and embedded rasters; the shell's icon and manifest links.
checks.fail: a broken asset or favicon reference on a load-bearing surface: High; whole icon-library imports or unsubset font families: Medium.

checks.title: Localization is real: strings in the catalog including `alt` and `aria-label`, ICU or `Intl.PluralRules` plurals, `Intl` date/number formatting, `dir` derived from locale, mirrored icons, expansion-safe layouts
checks.look: `locales/`, `*.po`, i18next/next-intl/vue-i18n config; hardcoded strings in templates; `grep -rn 'n === 1' src/`; `dir=` derivation.
checks.fail: an i18n runtime installed while screens hardcode English: High; hand-rolled plurals or fixed-locale formatting: Medium. Absent surface: record the absence with reason.

checks.title: Native UI uses native primitives: `Pressable`/`TextInput` over tap-handler `View`s, accessibility props on custom controls, `FlatList` with a stable `keyExtractor`, safe-area insets, explicit `Platform.select` branches
checks.look: `react-native` or `expo` in `package.json`; `.map()` renders inside `ScrollView`; `accessibilityLabel` and `accessibilityRole` density; web assumptions in shared code.
checks.fail: hundreds of rows mapped inside `ScrollView`, or custom controls without `accessibilityLabel`/`accessibilityRole`: High.

checks.title: No paper controls: every declared protection traces to real wiring: spinners to pending state, live regions to writers, skip links to existing ids, tokens to consumers, focus styles surviving the cascade
checks.look: each protection surfaced by A-UI-3 through A-UI-15, traced from declaration to the code path that exercises it.
checks.fail: a declared-but-unwired protection on a load-bearing surface (accessibility theater): Critical; elsewhere: High.

checks.title: The eight always-Critical conditions are absent: keyboard lockout, unnamed core control, inescapable focus trap, zoom disabled, captionless load-bearing media, a11y theater, no 320px reflow, broken load-bearing form
checks.look: the zero-count sweep `grep -rEn '<(div|span)[^>]*onClick|tabindex="[1-9]|user-scalable=no|maximum-scale=1' src/` plus the A-UI-3, A-UI-5, A-UI-11, and A-UI-12 reads.
checks.fail: any confirmed hit: Critical, never downgraded at any scale calibration. Plan-aware: a checked PLAN.mdx UI sweep task whose greps now fail also cross-references roadmap drift.

checks.title: Color independence and computable contrast: no meaning carried by color alone; statically computable token pairs meet 4.5:1
checks.look: error, success, and status styling that changes only color with no icon or text; the token file's foreground/background pairs computed numerically.
checks.fail: color-only signaling on forms or status surfaces: High; a token pair computing below 4.5:1: Medium at Tentative confidence (real pixel contrast needs a browser tool).

checks.title: The cascade is live: no never-matched `@media` blocks (breakpoint below the guarded element's own fixed min-width), no selectors matching nothing, no later rules silently defeating earlier protections
checks.look: media queries read against the fixed dimensions of the elements they guard; duplicated declarations; reset order relative to component styles.
checks.fail: a never-matched responsive block guarding a load-bearing adaptation: High; dead or duplicated CSS: Low. Dead JS/TS belongs to code-quality per the ownership map.

checks.title: Hydration and island wiring is deliberate: interactive islands carry client directives, no `use client` at route roots, no SSR/client markup divergence, lazy routes not statically imported anyway
checks.look: `grep -rn 'use client' src/app/` at route roots; Astro components with handlers but no `client:` directive; `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` in SSR render paths.
checks.fail: an interactive island shipped with no client directive (dead controls): High; a route-root `use client` or a lazy route still statically imported: Medium.

checks.title: WCAG 2.2 pointer target size and focus appearance: interactive targets meet the 24 by 24 CSS px minimum with adequate spacing (WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size Minimum), and the stated mobile target contract where one exists; the focus indicator meets the focus-appearance minimum (WCAG 2.4.11): never removed without an equivalent, with sufficient area and contrast against adjacent colors
checks.look: CSS for interactive controls' min-width/min-height/padding and spacing; `outline: none` or `outline: 0` with no replacement `:focus-visible` style; icon-only, close, or nav controls sized below 24px; dense list or toolbar tap targets.
checks.fail: interactive targets below the 24px minimum without a valid exception (inline text links, user-agent controls), or a removed or too-faint focus indicator: Medium (High when it blocks a primary action on touch or defeats keyboard focus visibility). Cross-reference F-CMP for WCAG 2.2 AA.

checks.title: Every route class has a deliberate indexation disposition: public content indexable, authed app routes, previews, and internal search noindexed. In plan-aware mode, diff against the plan's route-class matrix
checks.look: robots meta in layouts and `generateMetadata`, middleware `X-Robots-Tag`, per-route `robots` exports.
checks.fail: an indexable content route shipping noindex is Critical; authed or search-result routes with no index control is Medium.

checks.title: Body content, title, canonical, robots meta, and JSON-LD exist in the initial server HTML of every indexable route
checks.look: `ssr: false`, `"use client"` at content route roots, `react-helmet` with no server pass, `document.title =`, meta writes in `useEffect`, `prerender.io`, `rendertron`, `react-snap`.
checks.fail: CSR-only indexable content is Critical; any prerender-for-bots middleware is High.

checks.title: Navigation is crawlable and status codes are honest: real `<a href>` links, a hard 404 for unknown routes, no JS or meta-refresh redirects, paginated URLs behind infinite scroll
checks.look: hash routing `#/`, `onClick` navigation, `/* /index.html 200` in `_redirects`, `error_page 404 =200`, infinite-scroll components.
checks.fail: SPA catch-all serving 200 is High; hash-routed indexable content is High; infinite scroll with no paginated fallback is Medium.

checks.title: The robots.txt generator obeys the five constraints: no `Disallow: /` under `User-agent: *` in prod, no dead directives (`noindex:`, `crawl-delay`, `host:`), no blocking of `/_next/`, `/static/`, `*.js`, `*.css`, an absolute `Sitemap:` line, and robots.txt never used as an index control
checks.look: `app/robots.ts`, `public/robots.txt`, robots plugins and their env branches.
checks.fail: prod `Disallow: /` is Critical; a Disallowed URL that also carries noindex is High; dead directives are Low.

checks.title: The environment indexability guard fails safe (unset env resolves to noindex, prod never emits it) and CI asserts prod builds contain no noindex
checks.look: env comparisons around noindex output, `VERCEL_ENV` or `DEPLOY_ENV` reads, CI build-integrity steps.
checks.fail: inverted or missing polarity that can noindex prod is Critical; multiple deploy envs with no guard or no CI assertion is High.

checks.title: The sitemap derives from the content model: only canonical, indexable, 200-status URLs, real per-URL lastmod, chunked above 50000 URLs, no `priority` or `changefreq`
checks.look: sitemap source queries, lastmod assignment, chunking logic.
checks.fail: noindexed or redirected URLs emitted is Medium; a uniform build-time lastmod is Medium; `priority`/`changefreq` is Low theater.

checks.title: Crawl traps consolidate: faceted navigation, sort orders, and session params canonicalize or noindex to the clean base URL; pagination is crawlable and never canonicalizes to page 1

checks.look: H2 headings of each present pillar body.
