checks.title: Decisions entries state rationale consistent with reality; the audit checks each against manifests and git history (pillars-verify 3e)
checks.look: Decisions sections; the dependency the decision names must exist; "chose Postgres over SQLite for write throughput" while the lockfile ships `better-sqlite3` is drift.
checks.fail: a Decision contradicted by the current tree or dependency set. Medium.

checks.title: Rules pass earn-your-keep (state only constraints not inferable from code) and the code obeys them (pillars-verify 3f)
checks.look: each Rule; grep the codebase for violations ("no raw SQL outside `db/raw.ts`" -> `grep -rn 'unsafe(' src/ --include='*.ts'` outside that file).
checks.fail: code violating a declared Rule: Medium. A Rule restating an inferable fact ("we use Drizzle"): Low.

checks.title: Context claims match the code: stack and version claims against manifests, file-location claims against the tree, convention claims against 3-5 sampled files (flag at 2 or more failures in 5)
checks.look: every declarative Context claim in every present pillar, per the pillars-verify 3a-3d priority order.
checks.fail: any drifted claim (declared `src/db/schema.ts`, actual `app/lib/db/schema.ts`; declared Drizzle, lockfile has Prisma). Medium; High when the drifted claim misleads on auth, data handling, or deploy targets.

checks.title: Content sits in its owning pillar per the boundary tiebreakers: secrets guidance in `config.md` not `auth.md`, product analytics apart from system observe, layout in `repo.md` vs module shape in `arch.md`, CLI UX in `cli.md` not `ui.md`; cross-boundary references go through Touchpoints, not duplicated prose
checks.look: grep pillar bodies for the tiebreaker concerns; diff near-identical paragraphs across pillars.
checks.fail: misplaced content or the same guidance duplicated in two pillars. Low.

checks.title: The coupling graph is clean per scope: no pillar exceeds 3 `must_read_with` entries, no shared dependency hides across 3 or more pillars unpromoted, `always_load: true` is limited to the floor, and dependencies never leak across scope boundaries
checks.look: all frontmatter grouped by scope; count `must_read_with` lengths and cross-pillar repeats; resolve every hard and soft reference locally; `grep -c 'always_load: true' agents//*.md`.
checks.fail: any of the four smells above. Low.

checks.title: No stale exclusions: every `excluded:` name still has zero code in its area
checks.look: each exclusion against the tree (`ui` excluded but `src/components/` holds 30 files; `observe` excluded but a `sentry` dependency landed).
checks.fail: code present in an excluded area with no pillar authored. Medium.

checks.title: Tool-native instruction files are one-line redirects to `AGENTS.md` and `./agents/`, never parallel instruction documents
checks.look: every file found under the tool-native globs in the surface map; count non-empty lines; diff any rules content against the pillar tree.
checks.fail: a fork carrying its own rules alongside `AGENTS.md` (two memories drifting independently). Medium; Low when the fork's content is identical boilerplate.

checks.title: Structural and routing validation runs in CI: a validator checks frontmatter, headings, identities, selectors, floors, exclusions, local catalogs, references, budgets, nested scopes, and representative task-to-load-set fixtures, and fails the build on ERROR
checks.look: `scripts/validate_pillars.py`, `grep -rn 'validate_pillars' .github/workflows/`.
checks.fail: pillar tree present but no CI job validates it. Low.

checks.title: Maintenance is real, not aspirational: pillars update alongside the code they describe; Gaps entries answered by code are removed; Watchouts accumulate where incident evidence exists
checks.look: `git log --follow -1 --format=%cs` per pillar vs latest commits touching its domain paths; Gaps entries against the tree.
checks.fail: a pillar untouched across a refactor that changed its domain, or a Gaps question the code has long since answered. Medium.

checks.title: The memory system is operable end to end: the portable matcher and depth-1 protocol compute deterministic scope-labeled load, primary, and absent sets with no contradictions. Plan-aware: PLAN.mdx's Agent memory section states the protocol as binding
checks.look: compute representative load sets; cross-check exclusions against files and catalogs; validate status values; inspect stub bodies; apply outer-to-inner scope precedence and child exclusions.
checks.fail: any loader/tree contradiction that makes the protocol ambiguous. Medium.

checks.title: (audit-only) Non-Pillars memory is graded on equivalents, not zeroed: a lone `CLAUDE.md`, `.cursor/rules/`, or copilot-instructions file is audited for accuracy against code (as A-MEM-11), size discipline, and single-source-of-truth (as A-MEM-15); record the convention in the audit and re-scope A-MEM-2 through A-MEM-8 to it
checks.look: the classified tool-native files from the surface map; line counts; claim spot-checks.
checks.fail: a monolithic instruction file past roughly 300 lines mixing all domains with drifted claims and no routing. Medium.

checks.title: (audit-only) No unsafe instruction content: memory must not direct agents to bypass verification or safety ("skip tests", "force-push to main", "auto-approve all commands", piping remote scripts to shell) and must not embed credentials (the secret itself is cross-referenced to `F-SEC` per the ownership map; the instruction placement is the MEM finding)
checks.look: `grep -rniE 'force-push|--no-verify|skip (the )?tests|curl .*\| *(ba)?sh' AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md agents/ .cursor/ 2>/dev/null`.
checks.fail: an instruction a compliant agent would follow into destructive or unreviewable action. High.

checks.title: (audit-only) Portable routing is executable and stable: representative tasks preserve the deterministic ASCII minimum result even when a semantic matcher adds recall
checks.look: task-to-load-set fixtures, or generate tasks for each trigger, direct dependency, conditional `see_also`, and unrelated concern; compare two independent runs.
checks.fail: normalized selector collisions, substring matching (`api` matching `capital`), unstable ordering, transitive dependency expansion, or a fixture mismatch. Medium.

checks.title: (audit-only) Nested scope inheritance is coherent: applicable scopes resolve root to leaf, nearest guidance wins conflicts, child exclusions suppress inherited routed pillars, and references remain local to their declaring scope
checks.look: every nested `AGENTS.md` plus adjacent `agents/`; compute load sets for paths inside and outside each child scope.
checks.fail: a child task silently ignores ancestor guidance, an ancestor overrides the child, a child exclusion leaves the inherited pillar active, or a dependency resolves across scopes. Medium.

checks.title: (audit-only) Local absent catalogs are internally consistent and truthful: version 1, unique valid identities, required triggers, no present or excluded duplicates, no floor identities, and every entry still absent
checks.look: every `agents/catalog.yaml` against local pillar paths, local exclusions, and live code surfaces.
checks.fail: malformed catalog or present/excluded/floor conflict: Medium; an entry left absent after its pillar was created: Low.

checks.title: (audit-only) Routed context stays within the Pillars 1.1 budgets unless a specific exception is documented: always-loaded files at most 1,000 words and 8 KiB, scope floor at most 2,000 words and 16 KiB, task-routed files at most 2,000 words and 16 KiB
checks.look: byte and word counts per pillar and per scope; documented exception beside each overage.
checks.fail: undocumented always-loaded overage: Medium because every task pays it; routed overage: Low unless it causes recurrent irrelevant instruction loading.

checks.title: Tier coherence. Reconstruct the type x stage x audience triple from signals and check the file inventory sits in its band (MVP 5-8 files, Growth 12-18, Enterprise 20-30 plus)
checks.look: root inventory count; contributor count, releases, and CI reality from the fingerprint; any pinned tier in docs or the plan.
checks.fail: inventory out of band for the evident stage in either direction (community pack on a solo MVP, funded product missing Growth files). Medium.

checks.title: Brownfield layering respected. Scaffold generations coexist coherently and an existing Pillars standard survived
checks.look: `AGENTS.md` plus `agents/*.md` with `pillar:` frontmatter still cross-referenced; duplicate or contradictory configs from different scaffold passes; plan-aware: the plan's mode line and stop-describe-propose protocol.
checks.fail: Pillars orphaned or clobbered by a regenerated `AGENTS.md`, or paired configs that contradict each other. Medium. Content quality of `AGENTS.md` belongs to agent-memory: cross-reference F-MEM.

checks.title: No destructive git automation. No script, hook, CI step, or task runner target runs destructive git against the working tree
checks.look: `grep -rEn 'git (reset --hard|checkout --|clean -f|push .*--force)' scripts/ Makefile Justfile .github/ .githooks/ package.json`.
checks.fail: destructive command reachable from automation, High; bare `--force` instead of `--force-with-lease` in a release workflow, Medium.

checks.title: Stack-derived artifacts. Every tooling file traces to the stack in the manifest
checks.look: `.gitignore` vs stack build dirs (`node_modules/`, `__pycache__/`, `target/`, `.next/`); each config file vs an installed dependency or built-in tool.
checks.fail: `.gitignore` missing the stack's build dir, Medium; a config for an uninstalled tool (`eslint.config.js` with no eslint dep, `.npmrc` in a Python repo), Medium.

checks.title: Polyglot layering (conditional). With more than one language: a primary is evident, tooling is layered per language, CI runs one job per language, and shared types generate from one source
checks.look: multiple manifests; the job list in the CI workflow; hand-written twin types on both sides of the API boundary.
checks.fail: one blended CI job across languages, or duplicated hand-rolled boundary types. Medium.

checks.title: No placeholders. Committed repo docs carry real values everywhere a template would stub
checks.look: `grep -rEn '\{\{|TODO|TBD|PLACEHOLDER|INSERT|example\.com' README.md LICENSE CONTRIBUTING.md SECURITY.md .github/`.
checks.fail: unresolved template variable, `example.com` contact, or wrong year or author in LICENSE. High in LICENSE and SECURITY.md, Medium elsewhere. Stubbed app code (TODO handlers, fake data) belongs to build: cross-reference per the ownership map.

checks.title: README proof test. README contains a description, install steps, a usage example, a license reference, and a Quick Start of 5 or fewer commands a fresh clone can run
checks.look: `README.md` headings and fenced command blocks.
checks.fail: missing install or usage, Medium; a README that is only a title and badges, High.

checks.title: Documentation set to tier. At reconstructed tier 2 plus: CONTRIBUTING names the real branch model, setup, and test command; SECURITY.md carries a real contact and response timeline; CHANGELOG uses Keep a Changelog headings with an Unreleased section
checks.look: the three files; the convention actually used in `git log`; CODE_OF_CONDUCT only where the audience warrants it.
checks.fail: a tier-required file missing with no stated reason, Medium; present but hollow (CHANGELOG with only a heading, CONTRIBUTING describing a workflow git history contradicts), Medium.

checks.title: CI runs the real pipeline. CI triggers on `pull_request` and push to the default branch and runs the project's actual lint, type-check, test, and build commands
checks.look: `.github/workflows/*.yml`, `.gitlab-ci.yml`, `bitbucket-pipelines.yml`; steps matching `run: echo` or `run: true`.
checks.fail: no CI on a funded product, High; stub steps faking green, High; missing `pull_request` trigger, Medium.

checks.title: Quality tooling set. One linter, one formatter, a git-hook manager, `.editorconfig`, and `.gitattributes` containing `* text=auto`
checks.look: stack-native configs (`biome.json`, ruff in `pyproject.toml`, `.golangci.yml`, clippy); `.husky/`, `lefthook.yml`, or `.pre-commit-config.yaml` actually invoking them.
checks.fail: no linter at tier 2 plus, Medium; missing `.editorconfig` or `.gitattributes`, Low; hook manager installed but running nothing, Low.

checks.title: One tool per job. No two tools share a job anywhere in the repo
checks.look: ESLint plus Biome configs, Prettier plus Biome, Black plus Ruff format, `dependabot.yml` plus `renovate.json`, two release tools, two hook managers.
checks.fail: duplicate tools for one job, Medium; both actively wired into CI or hooks, the finding quotes both config paths.

checks.title: Security automation wired. At tier 2 plus: exactly one dependency bot with update grouping, a SAST workflow, secret scanning, and branch protection documented with its exact settings
checks.look: `.github/dependabot.yml` or `renovate.json` grouping blocks; `codeql.yml` or a semgrep step; a gitleaks step or platform scanning; protection settings in CONTRIBUTING or settings-as-code.
checks.fail: no dependency bot, Medium; no SAST on a funded product, Medium; bot without grouping, Low. Actual leaked secrets are F-SEC: cross-reference, never re-score.

checks.title: Release machinery consistent. One release tool, `vX.Y.Z` SemVer tags, and the same commit convention cited by the release tool, CONTRIBUTING, and the PR template
checks.look: release-please, semantic-release, or changesets config; `git tag -l 'v*'`; convention strings across the three artifacts; `git log --oneline -30` conformance.
checks.fail: the tool parses Conventional Commits while history does not conform, or the three artifacts cite different conventions. Medium.

checks.title: Tier boundary honesty. Tier 4 artifacts (signed commits, SBOM, provenance, ADRs, runbooks) are whole or absent, never half-started
checks.look: SBOM steps in workflows and where their output goes; `docs/adr/` contents; runbooks; enforcement behind each hardened claim.
checks.fail: a hardened artifact started but inert (SBOM generated then discarded, ADR dir holding only a template), Low; a README claim with no enforcing config, Medium.

checks.title: Agent-safety layer. `.claude/settings.json` denies destructive commands, `.githooks/pre-push` exits nonzero on force-push to protected branches, and `.gitleaks.toml` is wired into both pre-commit and CI
checks.look: the three files plus the `core.hooksPath` setup and CI step that activate them.
checks.fail: all three absent on a repo agents execute against, Medium as one finding; a file present but unwired (hooks path never set, gitleaks config with no CI step), Low.

checks.title: Cross-references closed. Every file a doc links to exists, and every badge maps to a configured service on the right repo and branch
checks.look: relative links in README and CONTRIBUTING vs `git ls-files`; badge URLs vs workflow names, platform, and repo slug.
checks.fail: link to a missing file, Low; badge rendering 404 or unknown, or pointing at another repo or branch, Medium.

checks.title: Single naming across artifacts. Each core concept keeps one spelling across directory, test file, endpoint, and README heading
checks.look: concept nouns across `src/` directories, test filenames, route paths, and doc headings.
checks.fail: two spellings for one concept across repo surfaces, Low. In-code identifier idiom belongs to style-genome: cross-reference F-DNA.

checks.title: No empty ceremony. No `.gitkeep`-only directories, no committed `.env`, no governance pack on a solo repo, no stock template text
checks.look: `find . -name .gitkeep`; `git ls-files | grep -E '(^|/)\.env$'`; `GOVERNANCE.md` vs the fingerprint contributor count; templates containing "A clear and concise description".
checks.fail: committed `.env`, Medium (live values inside escalate through an F-SEC cross-reference); the rest Low.

checks.title: Stated finish line. The repo names its own hygiene bar somewhere durable: a target tier, a Mode C band out of 42, or a prior audit report with a target
checks.look: tier statement in README or CONTRIBUTING, `AUDIT-REPORT.md`; plan-aware: the Repository section's numeric target band.
checks.fail: no stated bar on a funded product, Low; the auditor then scores against the reconstructed tier and says so in the domain summary.

checks.title: Upstream consistency. Repo artifacts agree with upstream decisions on disk instead of re-deciding
checks.look: CI commands and tooling vs `.stack-ready/DECISION.md` or the plan's stack section; workspace layout vs the architecture record.
checks.fail: repo tooling contradicts a recorded upstream decision with no drift note, Low; the code wins, the missing note is the finding.

checks.title: Version control reality (audit-only). The project is a git repository and the index is clean of artifacts
checks.look: `git rev-parse --short HEAD`; `git ls-files` for `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `__pycache__/`, `target/`; tracked files over 5 MB outside LFS.
checks.fail: no repository at all, High per `intake.md`; tracked build artifacts or vendored dependency trees, Medium; large binaries outside LFS, Low.

checks.title: Tests exist and CI runs them (audit-only). A real test suite exists and the CI test step executes it
checks.look: test globs (`*.test.*`, `*_test.go`, `tests/`, `spec/`); the test command CI runs; scripts that exit 0 printing "no tests".
checks.fail: no tests on a funded product, High; tests present but never wired into CI, Medium. Test quality belongs to code-quality: cross-reference per the ownership map.

checks.title: Delivery reality (audit-only, conditional). Runs only when the roadmap domain is excluded per `intake.md`: the repo's release story matches shipped reality
checks.look: latest `v*` tag vs the CHANGELOG top entry; commit recency vs activity claims in README; release automation that has never cut a release.
checks.fail: CHANGELOG and tags disagree, Low; release machinery configured but unused across 6 months of active commits, Low.

checks.title: Documentation profile completeness (audit-only). The documentation set matches the project's detected profile (product form, scale, and risk or regulatory overlays): the documents a comparable project of this shape needs are present, scaled to it. A prototype is not faulted for a missing business-continuity plan; a funded-product or enterprise system is expected to carry a PRD, architecture and ADRs, a test strategy, a deploy and rollback plan, an operations runbook, and release notes, and at enterprise or regulated scale an initiation brief (charter, business case, stakeholders and RACI), a requirements-traceability matrix, and a closeout with lessons. Documents the form makes irrelevant are recorded not-applicable with a reason
checks.look: the root-doc and `docs/` inventory against the form, scale, and risk profile in `intake.md`; presence of the governance documents at funded-product or enterprise scale; not-applicable markers with their reason.
checks.fail: a required document for the detected profile is absent with no not-applicable reason: Medium (High when a security-critical or regulated project lacks a threat model, privacy or DSAR records, or incident-response documentation).

checks.title: The 12 pre-flight answers are reconstructable from the repo: who uses it and for what job, entities, stack, persistence, auth model, permission matrix, route map, deploy target, responsive scope
checks.look: README, docs, schema files, manifests, `.godplans/STATE.md`; in plan-aware mode, the PLAN.mdx build sections themselves.
checks.fail: a foundational answer (persistence, auth, routes) that neither code nor any doc yields: Medium.

checks.title: Domain traps are encoded in the schema: money as integer cents or `Decimal` (never float), append-only audit tables where regulation applies, an explicit soft-delete or hard-delete stance
checks.look: `prisma/schema.prisma`, migrations, ORM models; grep `Float` or `double` on columns named `price|amount|total|balance`.
checks.fail: float-typed money columns: High, Critical when live payment flows exist; regulated data with no audit table: High. When the database pass already carries the same root cause, cross-reference the F-DB id instead of double-billing.

checks.title: User data lives in a real persistence layer: survives reload, consistent across two sessions, no in-memory store or fixture acting as the database
checks.look: server handlers and hooks; grep module-scope `let users = [`, `new Map(` caches serving CRUD, JSON fixture imports in server code.
checks.fail: any user-facing feature persisting to memory or a fixture: Critical.

checks.title: Each shipped feature is a complete vertical: schema, server CRUD with permission checks, client hooks, pages with states, an audit entry, tests
checks.look: map each nav-exposed feature to its entity, handlers, hooks, pages, and tests.
checks.fail: a nav-exposed feature missing its server layer or persisting nothing: High; missing its audit write where sibling features have one: Medium.

checks.title: No layer skew: the repo does not show the 80-percent-per-layer signature of horizontal building
checks.look: count schema entities vs endpoints vs wired pages; endpoints no client calls, models no endpoint serves.
checks.fail: two or more entities with schema and endpoints but no working page, or pages with no backing endpoint: High.

checks.title: Every interactive element completes its chain to a user-visible outcome; blocked CTAs are removed, disabled with a visible reason, or logged in `deferred-cta.md` with all five fields
checks.look: grep `onClick={() => {}}`, `<form` without `onSubmit` or `action=`, `useMutation` without `onSuccess` or invalidation, empty `<DialogContent>` shells; read `.production-ready/deferred-cta.md`.
checks.fail: half-wired CTA on a primary flow: High, elsewhere Medium; a deferred entry with a vague blocker such as "later": Low.

checks.title: Every nav link resolves to a registered route, and no route ships as a placeholder
checks.look: sidebar and header `href` values against the route registry; grep `Coming soon|Not implemented|WIP` in page files.
checks.fail: a nav link that 404s: High; a coming-soon page or permanently disabled nav item: Medium.

checks.title: Auth is real: bad credentials rejected, session stored and persisted, every protected route gated server-side, passwords hashed with argon2 or bcrypt
checks.look: auth handlers, middleware, session config; grep `argon2|bcrypt`, and the negative `md5|sha1|sha256` near password fields.
checks.fail: login accepting arbitrary credentials, or gating enforced only client-side: Critical; fast-hash or plaintext passwords: Critical at any scale.

checks.title: RBAC is server-enforced: at least two roles, every mutation checks its permission-matrix cell on the server; UI hiding is courtesy, not security
checks.look: mutation handlers and middleware for role checks; compare admin-only UI actions to their server handlers.
checks.fail: a mutation whose only permission check lives in the client: Critical; no reconstructable role matrix: Medium.

checks.title: The three threat answers (attacker gain, highest-blast-radius mutation, trust boundaries) are reconstructable, and the named boundaries exist in code
checks.look: ADRs, STATE.md, middleware, the mutation with the widest write scope.
checks.fail: no reconstructable threat stance on a funded product: Medium. Missing tenant predicates and injection are security's: cross-reference F-SEC per the ownership map.

checks.title: A visual identity was committed: tokens wired globally, at least one rendered component inherits `--color-primary`, icons come from an icon library, no unmodified default theme
checks.look: `DESIGN.md`, global stylesheet or Tailwind theme, token variables, component class usage.
checks.fail: unmodified shadcn, Radix, or MUI default theme user-visible: Medium; emojis as UI icons: Low. Conditional: skip when no first-party UI. Visual a11y is ui's: cross-reference F-UI.

checks.title: Async surfaces carry loading, empty, and error states; forms validate client and server side with inline errors; every mutation ends in a toast or banner; tables over 25 rows paginate, sort, and filter with URL-preserved state
checks.look: list pages for the three branches, form components, mutation hooks, table components.
checks.fail: server-side validation absent on a write path: High; silent mutations or missing error states: Medium.

checks.title: Each feature carries its tests in-slice: integration CRUD, permission-denial, axe on new pages, and a contract test for any cross-slice public signature
checks.look: `tests/` or `__tests__/` per feature; OpenAPI snapshot or zod round-trip tests where another feature imports the signature. CI wiring is repo's: cross-reference F-REPO.
checks.fail: a shipped feature with zero integration and zero permission tests: High; a consumed cross-slice signature with no contract test: Medium. Whole-suite absence is F-REPO's existence concern, cross-referenced; this check scores per-feature coverage only.

checks.title: Every manifest dependency resolved against the registry: a lockfile entry exists for each, no typo-shaped names
checks.look: `package.json` against `package-lock.json` (or ecosystem equivalent); names one edit away from popular packages (`@tansatck/react-query`).
checks.fail: a manifest dependency with no lockfile resolution: Critical (slopsquatting risk); no lockfile at all: High.

checks.title: The hardening block landed: CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, and Referrer-Policy headers; rate limiting on login, mutations, uploads, exports; heavy libraries code-split under a bundle budget
checks.look: `next.config.js`, middleware, helmet or equivalent, rate-limit call sites, dynamic `import()` for chart and editor libs.
checks.fail: no rate limit on login: High; the header set absent: Medium. Secrets in repo or bundle are security's: cross-reference F-SEC per the ownership map.

checks.title: Production paths pass the hollow check: no TODO or implement-later markers, no raw `console.log` (logger calls in catch blocks excluded), no hardcoded fake-data arrays in components, no `Math.random()` driving charts or KPIs, no `alert()` or `prompt()` UI, no `Example*` or `Demo*` components shipped
checks.look: the grep battery over `src/`, adapted per stack (`binding.pry`, `dd(`, `var_dump(`, `raise NotImplementedError`).
checks.fail: fake data or `Math.random()` rendered as real metrics: High; TODO or debug-print density in prod paths: Medium.

checks.title: Session-state artifacts exist and match the code: a STATE.md scoped to build facts, an ADR for each non-obvious architectural choice
checks.look: `.godplans/STATE.md` or `.production-ready/STATE.md`, `adr/` directories; sample two ADR claims against the code.
checks.fail: a multi-contributor repo with zero ADRs behind non-obvious choices: Medium; STATE.md contradicting code: Medium (code wins).

checks.title: If the repo presents as shipped (deploy config, release tags), the closure gates hold: zero live deferred-CTA entries, zero open questions, zero TODOs
checks.look: `deferred-cta.md` Status fields, the STATE.md open-questions block, the A-BUILD-16 grep results.
checks.fail: a released repo with entries still `deferred` or questions still `open`: Medium; blockers reading "later" or "soon": Low.

checks.title: Cross-cutting features (search, exports, notifications, theme toggle, audit-log viewer) query real data
checks.look: the search handler's data source, export endpoints, the audit-log viewer's table reads.
checks.fail: search over a hardcoded array, an export serving a static fixture, or an audit-log viewer reading a table nothing writes: High.

checks.title: In brownfield or replan mode, the keep-rewrite-discard inventory exists and every rewrite item shipped or was reclassified with a reason
checks.look: the PLAN.mdx inventory table (plan-aware); `legacy/`, `old/`, `*-v1` trees against the import graph.
checks.fail: unshipped rewrite items on a plan marked complete: Medium. Dead-code specifics are code-quality's: cross-reference F-CODE per the ownership map.

checks.title: No unguarded destructive commands in project scripts: `migrate reset`, `db push --accept-data-loss`, `rm -rf`, or `--force` flags runnable without a confirmation gate or environment guard
checks.look: `package.json` scripts, `Makefile`, `justfile`. Pipeline placement is deploy's: cross-reference F-DEPLOY when CI runs one.
checks.fail: a destructive script that can point at a non-local database without a guard: High; unguarded but provably local-only: Low.

checks.title: No orphaned routes: every registered page is reachable from nav, a link, or a documented deep link
checks.look: the route registry against all `href`, `Link`, and `router.push` references.
checks.fail: an orphaned scaffold page: Medium; an orphaned page exposing data without auth: cross-reference F-SEC.

checks.title: Verify every calendar date rests on capacity math: executor count, engineer-weeks per cycle, rotation share, and a serial-fraction estimate
checks.look: roadmap artifacts; grep `20[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}` for dates and `engineer-weeks`, `capacity` for the block.
checks.fail: dated commitments with no capacity block covering them: High. Appetites and bands only, no dates: pass.

checks.title: Verify a cadence model is declared by name as an ADR-shaped paragraph with two rejected alternatives and a re-evaluation trigger
checks.look: grep `Shape Up`, `quarterly`, `SAFe`, `PI`, `continuous`, `milestone-based` across roadmap artifacts.
checks.fail: no named cadence anywhere: Medium. Cadence named without alternatives or trigger: Low.

checks.title: Verify every scheduled row passes the three-label test: grounded commitment, outcome-framed direction, or owned open question; bare feature names banned
checks.look: every row and task title in the roadmap artifacts.
checks.fail: bare feature rows ("SSO", "dark mode") with no outcome, appetite, or label: Medium; quote the failing row in Evidence.

checks.title: Verify every phase and task anchors to an upstream artifact: a requirements id, a named architecture component, or a named external constraint
checks.look: Requirements lines and component references on tasks; external-constraint tags.
checks.fail: anchor-less speculative items: Medium; a schedule where most rows have no anchor is a wishlist: High.

checks.title: Verify the precision ceiling matches upstream quality: full decomposition only where requirements and architecture are complete; thin upstream labeled directional
checks.look: `.prd-ready/`, `.architecture-ready/`, or the plan's product and architecture sections versus decomposition depth.
checks.fail: a fully decomposed dated schedule atop absent or sketch-level upstream, unlabeled: Medium.

checks.title: Verify the task queue is topologically sorted and cycle-free: every Depends on line names an earlier task id or none
checks.look: walk all Depends on lines in queue order; detect forward references and cycles.
checks.fail: a task scheduled before its dependency, or any cycle: High (the queue is unexecutable as written).

checks.title: Verify the riskiest unknowns come first: every flagged hypothesis has a validation task (spike, prototype, measurement) before work that assumes it
checks.look: hypotheses in the Decisions section; `spikes/` with `Verdict:` lines; wave placement of validation tasks.
checks.fail: dependent work scheduled or shipped before the hypothesis validation: Medium.

checks.title: Verify parallelism stays within capacity: tracks per wave at or below executor count; `[P]` tasks have disjoint Files lists; an Amdahl note exists
checks.look: wave packing against the capacity block; intersect the Files lines of every `[P]` pair in a wave.
checks.fail: `[P]` tasks sharing files or more tracks than executors: Medium. Missing Amdahl note on a multi-executor plan: Low.

checks.title: Verify a named prioritization framework fits items to appetites, and oversized items were shrunk or deferred, never carried at full scope
checks.look: grep `RICE`, `ICE`, `WSJF`, `MoSCoW`, `Kano`, `appetite` in roadmap artifacts; deferral records.
checks.fail: no framework and items visibly carried past their appetite at full scope: Medium; framework absent but appetites honored: Low.

checks.title: Verify full phase anatomy: concrete name, binary Checkpoint gate, Must-haves block, anchored in-scope list, non-empty out-of-scope with reasons, rabbit holes, dependencies
checks.look: every phase block in the roadmap artifacts.
checks.fail: a non-binary gate ("improve X") or an empty out-of-scope list: Medium per pattern, root phase quoted.

checks.title: Verify the precision gradient: current cycle fully decomposed; later horizons decay to themes then outcomes; no day-level dates beyond cycle one; no bare single-point dates
checks.look: date resolution per horizon; decomposition depth per horizon.
checks.fail: day-level dates in later horizons or a bare single-point target date without band or appetite: Medium.

checks.title: Verify any launch block is complete: named mode, banded date, readiness gates each scheduled as dated tasks (observability-live, rollback-tested, runbooks-reviewed, support-briefed), a D-calendar with at least D-7, D-0, D+7, and a slip protocol
checks.look: launch sections; `docs/runbooks/rollback.md`; gate tasks in the queue.
checks.fail: a launch date with any gate task missing: High (paper launch gate). Gate substance cross-references F-DEPLOY and F-OBS per the ownership map.

checks.title: Verify launch is gated on hardening: the first launch task depends on the security findings check, and any open Critical carries a dated, named, time-bounded risk acceptance
checks.look: Depends on lines of launch tasks; risk-acceptance entries; this audit's own F-SEC blocks on re-audit.
checks.fail: launch scheduled or shipped past an open Critical with no complete risk acceptance: High. The finding substance stays with F-SEC.

checks.title: Verify the task list works as the downstream handoff: each build task carries owner or lane, appetite, anchors, Depends on, and phase; deploy cadence and rollback posture stated; Must-haves name KPIs
checks.look: task field completeness across the queue; the deploy phase statement.
checks.fail: tasks an executor cannot start without questions (missing Files, Acceptance, or Verify lines): Medium.

checks.title: Verify completion is artifact verification: every checked task's named artifacts exist non-empty on disk, every Verify line is an exact command, and the final phase is Verification
checks.look: `test -s` every artifact named by a `[x]` task's Must-haves and Files lines; read Verify lines for command shape.
checks.fail: a checked task whose named artifact is missing or empty: High (rubber-stamp done). Prose Verify lines that no shell can run: Medium.

checks.title: Verify upstream prerequisite guards: no phase started before its upstream artifacts existed, and upstream edits after downstream check-offs carry reconciliation notes
checks.look: `git log` dates on upstream artifacts versus the commits that flipped downstream boxes.
checks.fail: upstream artifact modified after dependent tasks were checked, no reconciliation note: Medium.

checks.title: Verify the ledger is complete: every domain or sibling appears as done, skipped (with reason), imported (with source), or failed (with note); failed Verify keeps the box unchecked with a dated note
checks.look: the seven-status vocabulary in canonical `.arc-ready/PROGRESS.md`, legacy `.kickoff-ready/PROGRESS.md`, or the plan's ledger; notes under unchecked tasks.
checks.fail: work absent from the ledger entirely (silence as status), or a checked box above a failure note: Medium.

checks.title: Verify governance is written down: review cadence, authority map per horizon, re-plan triggers, freeze conditions, archive rule, and per-session `updated:` bumps
checks.look: governance section; frontmatter `updated:` stamps against session log entries.
checks.fail: no review cadence or authority map on a multi-contributor repo: Medium; stale `updated:` stamp alone: Low (observed drift is A-ROAD-22).

checks.title: Verify the audience declaration and, if mixed, a redacted public derivative: no capacity math, no owner names, no rabbit-hole specifics, "won't" phrased as "Not planned"
checks.look: `docs/ROADMAP-PUBLIC.md`; grep it for `engineer-weeks`, owner names from git, rabbit-hole text copied from the internal file.
checks.fail: a public derivative leaking capacity math or owners, or the internal file designated public: High. Mixed audience with no derivative: Medium.

checks.title: Verify sign-off and retrospectives are scheduled work: attestation tasks before commitments hardened, and a retro artifact per completed cycle
checks.look: sign-off tasks in the queue; `docs/retrospectives/` against completed phases.
checks.fail: hardened commitments with no attestation record on a multi-contributor repo, or a completed cycle with no retro: Low.

checks.title: Verify the plan matches the code (audit-only; this is the plan-drift check the ownership map assigns here): decisions the code contradicts, checked feature tasks with no code trace, requirements with no trace in source, bulk check-off commits
checks.look: plan decisions versus the intake fingerprint; `git log -p` on the plan file for many boxes flipped in one commit with no matching work commits.
checks.fail: a plan decision the code contradicts or a checked feature with no code path: High. Bulk check-offs in a single commit: Medium.

checks.title: Verify freshness against delivery and the arc artifact dependency chain (audit-only): the roadmap moved while the repo moved, and downstream artifacts were reconciled after upstream changes
checks.look: `git log -1 --format=%ci` on each roadmap and arc artifact; compare upstream and downstream modification times; compare `.launch-ready/PREPUBLICATION.md` against `.harden-ready/FINDINGS.md`.
checks.fail: a cadence cycle of commits (or 50 or more commits) since the roadmap was last touched: Medium; an upstream artifact newer than a checked downstream tier with no reconciliation note: Medium; prepublication evidence older than hardening changes: High.

checks.title: Verify one source of truth and a complete arc ledger (audit-only): multiple roadmap artifacts agree on what is done and what is next, or a canonical pointer names the live one. Every claimed arc tier has a non-empty artifact and every present artifact appears in the ledger
checks.look: cross-compare status and sequence across `ROADMAP.md`, PLAN.mdx, `.arc-ready/PROGRESS.md`, legacy PROGRESS.md, and tracker exports; inventory the arc artifact chain.
checks.fail: two artifacts disagreeing on status or sequence with no canonical pointer: Medium; a claimed artifact missing or empty, an artifact absent from the ledger, invalid status vocabulary, or dependency-order violation: Medium.

checks.title: Same-artifact promotion. One build step produces one content-addressed artifact promoted unchanged; per-region platform rebuilds record a source commit and build config pin as the artifact identity
checks.look: deploy workflows; count `docker build` and bundler build invocations; build steps inside promote jobs; image tags (digest or SHA vs branch names).
checks.fail: the pipeline rebuilds per environment, or promotes with no pinned identity. High.

checks.title: Artifact and config separation. The artifact carries no environment-specific values and no build-time secrets
checks.look: Dockerfile `ARG`, `ENV`, and `COPY` of `.env` files; build args named like credentials; per-environment bundles baking `API_URL` at build time.
checks.fail: env-specific values or secrets baked into the artifact. High. Secret literal values in git or CI belong to security: cross-reference F-SEC.

checks.title: Change classification discipline. Shipped changes are classified reversible, data-forward, mixed, or side-effectful somewhere durable
checks.look: PR template, deploy docs, `.deploy-ready/` state; plan-aware: the plan's per-task change classes against what actually shipped.
checks.fail: the repo ships migrations and side-effectful code (email sends, payment captures) with no classification convention anywhere. Medium.

checks.title: Class-matched rollback artifacts. Reversible changes carry a revert command with time-to-revert; data-forward changes carry a compensating-forward plan plus restore point; side-effectful changes carry idempotency guards
checks.look: `docs/deploy/rollback.md`, `.deploy-ready/rollback.md`, runbooks; idempotency keys at webhook and payment call sites.
checks.fail: "rollback: redeploy previous image" adjacent to a schema migration, High; a deployed product with no rollback doc at all, High.

checks.title: Expand/contract calendar. Data-forward changes decompose into expand, migrate, cutover, and contract as separate deploys
checks.look: migration files for single-migration rename or drop-and-replace; `docs/deploy/migration-calendar.md`, `.deploy-ready/migrations/`.
checks.fail: expand and contract in one migration on a populated table, High; expand shipped, contract unscheduled, no expand-only-by-design reason, Medium.

checks.title: Migration guardrail forms. Migrations on populated tables use the lock-safe forms
checks.look: `migrations/` for single-step `NOT NULL`, `ALTER COLUMN` type changes, `CREATE INDEX` without `CONCURRENTLY`, missing `lock_timeout` and `statement_timeout`, backfills inside the DDL transaction, renames of columns still read by running code.
checks.fail: any form taking an ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on a populated table, High. Schema shape and transaction correctness belong to database: cross-reference F-DB; this check owns lock behavior at deploy time only.

checks.title: Promotion ladder and parity. The rungs from build to prod are named, each with five parity properties: traffic source, data source, scale, flag defaults, observability reach
checks.look: `environment:` keys in workflows, per-environment platform config, `docs/deploy/topology.md`.
checks.fail: multiple environments in workflows with no parity documentation anywhere, Medium; a funded product deploying straight to prod with no non-prod rung, High.

checks.title: Eight gates in order. Build, test, artifact security scan, promote non-prod, promote staging, approval, promote prod, post-deploy verification
checks.look: deploy workflow job graph and `needs:` edges; scan steps (trivy, grype, secret scan) pointed at the artifact, not just the source.
checks.fail: a single job that builds, tests, and deploys on push to main, High; no artifact scan gate, Medium. Test existence itself is repo territory: cross-reference F-REPO.

checks.title: Approval by mechanism. Shipping to prod is a choice enforced by tooling, never a side effect of committing
checks.look: `environment:` with a protection rule, tag-triggered or `workflow_dispatch` prod deploys, deploy-marker conventions for solo builders.
checks.fail: prod deploy fires on every push to main with no protection rule and no distinct second action. High.

checks.title: Secrets path and identity split. Runtime-only injection; least-privileged runtime identity distinct from the deploy identity
checks.look: `pull_request_target` with fork checkout and secret access; secret-shaped literals in workflow YAML; deploy credentials on a personal access token; IaC where the runtime role equals the deploy role.
checks.fail: `pull_request_target` untrusted checkout with secrets, Critical; PAT-on-personal-account credential or no identity split, High. Leaked secret values are F-SEC: cross-reference, never re-bill.

checks.title: Rollout strategy and the four-field canary. Strategy named per change; every canary carries a named metric, numeric threshold, bounded window, and automated rollback trigger
checks.look: rollout config (`strategy:` blocks, Argo Rollouts, Flagger, `bluegreen` in `fly.toml`); canary claims in docs and job names vs wired analysis steps.
checks.fail: canary claimed with any of the four fields missing, High (paper canary); an untested change reaching 100 percent of user traffic in one uniform step, Medium. The metric pipeline itself belongs to observe: cross-reference F-OBS.

checks.title: Cold-start evidence. First deploys to an environment walk the ten-gate checklist: reachability, DNS, TLS validity over 30 days, migrations through expand, env vars set, IAM role, registry pull, truthful healthcheck, logs reaching the env, rollback dry-run
checks.look: `docs/deploy/cold-start-*.md`, `.deploy-ready/` checklists; environments newly added to workflows.
checks.fail: a new environment appears in workflows with no cold-start record, Medium; no checklist artifact on a pre-first-deploy repo, Low.

checks.title: Truthful readiness probe. The probe fails while a critical dependency is down; never 200-on-bind
checks.look: handlers behind `/health`, `/ready`, `/healthz`; whether the body exercises the database or a critical dependency; `readinessProbe` targets in manifests.
checks.fail: an unconditional 200 handler wired as the readiness probe in a service with a database. High.

checks.title: Flag lineage. No flag name is reused until the old code path behind it is confirmed removed
checks.look: flag definition sites and provider config; git history of flag names; dormant code behind long-lived flags.
checks.fail: a reused flag name whose prior gated code path still exists in the tree (the Knight Capital shape). High.

checks.title: Post-deploy verification. Prod deploys are followed by healthcheck, a latency and error-rate window of at least 15 minutes, a critical-path smoke run, and contract scheduling when an expand shipped
checks.look: workflow steps after the prod deploy step: smoke jobs, `curl -sf` polls, verification scripts.
checks.fail: a prod deploy job with zero verification steps after it. Medium.

checks.title: Rehearsal and incident log. Revert commands run against a non-prod copy within 90 days of first deploy; every rollback or close call writes an incident file
checks.look: rehearsal timestamps in rollback docs; `.deploy-ready/incidents/`, `docs/incidents/`.
checks.fail: a product deployed over 90 days (per tags and releases) with no rehearsal evidence and no incident log location. Medium.

checks.title: Handoff artifacts. Topology doc, healthcheck inventory, per-service rollback doc, and migration calendar exist and agree with the workflows
checks.look: `docs/deploy/`, `.deploy-ready/`; environment names in docs vs environment names in workflows.
checks.fail: a deployed product carrying none of the four, Medium (one finding listing every gap); docs contradicting the workflows, Medium with both sides quoted.

checks.title: Destructive command gating. `terraform destroy`, `kubectl delete`, `prisma migrate reset`, and `rm -rf` never reach production without explicit confirmation and a named, verified restore point
checks.look: workflows and `scripts/` for destructive commands in prod contexts; `--force` and `--auto-approve` flags on deploy paths.
checks.fail: a destructive command reachable against a production resource without a confirmation gate and named backup. Critical.

checks.title: Rollback targetability (audit-only). Production references immutable artifact identities so the previous version is always addressable
checks.look: `image:` tags in manifests and deploy steps; `:latest` or branch-name tags on prod paths; registry retention notes.
checks.fail: prod deploy pinned to `:latest` or another mutable tag. High (rollback has no target).

checks.title: Deploy concurrency control (audit-only). Two merges cannot interleave deploys to the same environment
checks.look: `concurrency:` groups on deploy workflows; queue or lock mechanisms in deploy scripts.
checks.fail: a prod deploy workflow with no concurrency group or equivalent lock. Medium.

checks.title: Pipeline-as-code reality (audit-only). The deploy path lives in the repo and changes to it are reviewed like code
checks.look: deploy workflows and configs present vs deployment evidence (prod URL in README, releases, badges) with no deploy path on disk.
checks.fail: a demonstrably deployed product whose only deploy path is click-ops. High.

checks.title: Service and journey inventory truth. A durable inventory names every service with its topology type and every user journey, and it matches the code
checks.look: `.observe-ready/STATE.md`, `observability/SLOS.md`, `docs/topology.md` vs the fingerprint's entry points, workers, and consumers; plan-aware: the plan's service table.
checks.fail: no inventory anywhere, or rows contradicting code (an SLO for a deleted service, a live worker absent from the register). Medium.

checks.title: Three-lane metric classification. Every charted or alerted metric is an SLI with an SLO, a diagnostic marked non-alerting, or gone
checks.look: dashboard panel queries and alert expressions vs the SLO register; lane or `non_alerting` markers in `observability/`.
checks.fail: a chart or alert on a metric bound to no SLO and carrying no diagnostic marker. Medium.

checks.title: SLO completeness. Each named journey has one SLI with a measurement query, one target, one window, one computed error budget
checks.look: the SLO register; OpenSLO or sloth yaml; terraform SLO resources.
checks.fail: a deployed user-facing journey with no SLO anywhere, High; a row missing query, window, or budget, Medium.

checks.title: Error-budget policy. Every SLO carries a four-field policy: Trigger, Action, Stakeholder, Exit
checks.look: policy blocks adjacent to SLO rows in the register.
checks.fail: an SLO with no policy is a paper SLO. Medium, and the journey is not counted as promised.

checks.title: Feasibility ceiling. No SLO promises above the product of its upstream availabilities
checks.look: the dependency graph from the fingerprint; vendor SLA tiers in IaC and the register's feasibility math.
checks.fail: a target above the computed ceiling with no named mitigation (redundancy, degradation path). Medium.

checks.title: Low-traffic strategy. Journeys with fewer requests than 10x the error budget per window declare extended windows, synthetic traffic, or aggregation
checks.look: traffic notes in the register; synthetic check configs (`checkly`, `blackbox_exporter`, cron pingers).
checks.fail: burn-rate alerting on a trickle journey with no declared strategy. Low.

checks.title: Burn-rate paging. Every PAGE derives from an SLI burn rate with at least two windows; cause signals never page
checks.look: PAGE-severity rules in `infra/alerts/`, Prometheus rules, terraform monitors, Grafana alerting.
checks.fail: a PAGE on CPU, memory, disk, or raw error count, High; a single-window burn alert, Medium.

checks.title: Runbook link and owner per page. Every PAGE rule carries a specific runbook URL (not a directory) and a named owner
checks.look: `runbook_url` and `owner` fields in each PAGE rule and in `.observe-ready/alerts.md`.
checks.fail: a PAGE with no specific runbook URL or no owner. High.

checks.title: Deadman switches and out-of-band routing. Every silent path alerts on absence, and paging does not depend on the paged system
checks.look: heartbeat emitters in scheduled jobs and consumers; `absent()` or deadman rules; routing config (direct pager vs a Slack workspace tied to the app's SSO).
checks.fail: a prod worker, cron, or pipeline with no deadman alert, High; alert routing that dies with the observed system, High.

checks.title: Structured log contract. One JSON format with `trace_id`, `service`, `level`, `timestamp`, `event`; correlation IDs cross every call type; retention stated per log class
checks.look: the shared logger module; queue producers and consumers for ID pass-through; collector or vendor retention config.
checks.fail: services on ad-hoc `console.log` or `print` logging, `trace_id` dying at an async boundary, or retention unstated. Medium. PII in logs: F-SEC.

checks.title: Trace propagation and sampling. OTel context crosses every boundary in the graph; SLO-backed services sample tail-based or error-biased; retention covers the SLO window; span names low-cardinality
checks.look: `instrumentation.*` bootstrap; sampler settings (`TraceIdRatioBased` values, `ParentBased`); collector `tail_sampling` processor; retention config.
checks.fail: head-only 1 percent sampling on an SLO service, retention shorter than the SLO window, or context lost at a queue or cold start. Medium.

checks.title: Error tracking with release tags. Every service reports exceptions with `release` bound to the deploy identifier, plus a grouping-tuning trace
checks.look: `Sentry.init` and equivalents; `release:` wired to a git SHA or deploy env var; grouping notes in `.observe-ready/`.
checks.fail: no error tracker on a deployed service, High; a tracker with no release tag, Medium. Scrubber config: F-SEC.

checks.title: Cardinality budget. High-cardinality dimensions stay off per-series priced backends, with the exclusion list written down
checks.look: label and tag sets in instrumentation code (`user_id`, `request_id`, `session_id`, `tenant_id`, `order_id`); the backend pricing model from the stack fingerprint; `observability/CARDINALITY.md`.
checks.fail: a high-cardinality label on a per-series backend with no exclusion rule. High.

checks.title: Above-the-fold discipline. The primary dashboard leads with the burn-rate gauge and holds at most 7 above-fold charts, each bound to an SLO, SLI, or named diagnostic
checks.look: panel count, order, and queries in `grafana/dashboards/*.json` or `infra/dashboards/` vs the register.
checks.fail: over 7 above-fold charts, first chart not the burn-rate gauge, or unbound panels above the fold. Medium.

checks.title: Artifact ownership. Every dashboard and alert carries owner and last_reviewed; at most 3 dashboards per service
checks.look: metadata fields or the catalogs `.observe-ready/dashboards.md` and `.observe-ready/alerts.md`.
checks.fail: orphan artifacts, or a fourth dashboard with no stated reason. Low.

checks.title: Surface independence. Dashboards, alert routing, runbooks, status page, trace store, and on-call schedule are each reachable when the observed service is down
checks.look: `observability/INDEPENDENCE.md`; `grafana`, `prometheus`, `loki`, `jaeger` services inside the app's own compose file or cluster manifests; status page and runbook hosting.
checks.fail: any of the six rows hosted on the observed system with no remediation and no justified exception. High.

checks.title: Executable runbooks. One runbook per PAGE with fenced commands using exact flags, hosted out-of-band, executed within 90 days
checks.look: `runbooks/*.md` for command fences and `last_executed:` dates; a tabletop cadence entry on the calendar file.
checks.fail: prose-only runbooks ("check the logs"), or `last_executed` absent or older than 90 days. Medium.

checks.title: Incident loop. A SEV-1/2/3 ladder with response expectations, an IC rotation (or named solo responder), a status-page cadence, and comms templates exist
checks.look: `observability/INCIDENTS.md`, `docs/incident*`, status page config, comms templates.
checks.fail: none of the four exists for a deployed product. Medium at funded-product, Low below.

checks.title: Learning loop. The post-mortem template forces at least one observability-gap action item with a due date; a quarterly alert-prune cadence is recorded
checks.look: `observability/templates/post-mortem.md`, incident files with tracked action items, the prune log.
checks.fail: incidents on record with no post-mortems, or a template missing the gap-item or due-date field. Low.

checks.title: Bright line and watchlist. Conversion analytics stay out of the ops surface; SLOs missing a policy or query stay visible on a paper-SLO watchlist
checks.look: business metrics (signup conversion, MRR, DAU) in ops dashboards or alert rules; the watchlist in the register or `.observe-ready/STATE.md`.
checks.fail: a conversion metric paging or holding an above-fold ops slot, Low; paper SLOs silently counted as promised fold into the A-OBS-4 finding.

checks.title: Log level honesty (audit-only). Levels mean what they claim: ERROR is actionable, DEBUG is off in prod
checks.look: logger call sites (stack traces at WARN, routine ops at ERROR, everything at INFO); prod config enabling DEBUG.
checks.fail: ERROR used for non-actionable noise or DEBUG on in prod, Low; Medium when the misleveled stream feeds an alert.

checks.title: Alert wiring liveness (audit-only). Every notification route actually delivers
checks.look: placeholder webhook URLs (`example.com`, `hooks.slack.com/services/XXX`), committed silences without expiry, `enabled: false` monitors, receivers unbound in `alertmanager.yml`.
checks.fail: a PAGE route pointing at a placeholder or disabled receiver, High; dead TICKET routes, Medium.

checks.title: Dark entry points (audit-only). Every entry point in the fingerprint emits some telemetry
checks.look: routes, consumers, and jobs from the fingerprint vs middleware coverage, span creation, and logger imports per module.
checks.fail: a user-facing route or prod worker with zero metrics, spans, and structured logs. Medium.

checks.title: Phantom signals (audit-only). Alerts and charts reference metrics the code actually emits
checks.look: metric names in alert expressions and dashboard queries vs emission sites (`prom-client` registries, `statsd` calls, OTel meters).
checks.fail: a PAGE rule on a metric with no emission site (it can never fire), High; phantom dashboard panels, Medium.

checks.title: Positioning exists and survives the substitution test: the four sentences (who it is for, what it replaces, what it does differently, the differentiator test) are recoverable from a positioning doc or the landing hero, each falsified by swapping in at least two named competitors. In plan-aware mode, diff against the plan's positioning block
checks.look: `docs/launch/POSITIONING.md`, `.launch-ready/POSITIONING.md`, hero headline and sub-headline in the landing source.
checks.fail: a hero that stays plausible under a competitor-name swap is High (hero fatigue); no positioning artifact at funded-product scale is Medium.

checks.title: Tone and founder voice hold: three adjectives plus three anti-adjectives recorded, the hero in first person with a named founder or a founder-story line, no company-we voice above the fold
checks.look: the positioning doc's tone block; grep `we are thrilled|our team is passionate|our mission` across landing copy.
checks.fail: a company-we hero with no named founder is Medium; tone words absent while copy surfaces multiply is Low.

checks.title: Launch mode and tier are declared and scope the assets: a mode (A pre-launch through E quiet-B2B) and target tier recorded, channel assets consistent with it, and Mode C or D naming the dominant prior-launch failure cause
checks.look: `.launch-ready/STATE.md`, `docs/launch/`, `retrospectives/`.
checks.fail: broad-channel drafts in a declared Mode E repo is Medium; Mode C or D with no named prior failure cause is Medium; no mode recorded anywhere launch assets exist is Low.

checks.title: Landing anatomy: five sections in order (hero, social proof, feature grid, pricing if applicable, CTA), exactly one `<h1>`, exactly one primary CTA above the 1366x768 fold
checks.look: the landing route source, section markup, CTA components, hero media.
checks.fail: multiple above-the-fold primary CTAs is Medium; multiple `<h1>` elements is Medium; a carousel or autoplay-video hero is Medium.

checks.title: Feature grid honesty: 3-6 tiles, each naming a product-specific capability that exists in the codebase; no category-label tiles (Fast, Secure, Scalable) and no tile promising a feature no route or module delivers
checks.look: feature-grid markup diffed against the intake fingerprint's route and module inventory.
checks.fail: a tile promising vapor is High (cross-reference F-BUILD when the feature exists as a stub, per the ownership map); a grid over 6 tiles or built of category labels is Medium.

checks.title: Banned-word audit: zero hits above the fold from the slop set (seamless, powerful, revolutionary, effortless, intelligent, cutting-edge, game-changing, unlock, supercharge, streamline, empower, elevate, robust, best-in-class, leading, enterprise-grade, world-class)
checks.look: case-insensitive grep across landing source, email templates, and channel post drafts.
checks.fail: three or more hits above the fold is High (the AI-slop signature); one or two is Medium; hits in email subject lines is Medium.

checks.title: Copy voice: active voice, second person, named subjects, and no AI self-reference in the hero unless the product is an AI product whose differentiator is the AI itself
checks.look: hero and sub-hero copy; grep `AI-powered|powered by AI` in the hero block.
checks.fail: AI self-reference in a non-AI product's hero is Medium; passive third-person hero copy is Low.

checks.look: landing CSS and token files, tailwind config overrides, icon imports (lucide, heroicons, phosphor), hero image assets.
checks.fail: emoji as UI markers is Medium; an unmodified template palette and typeface on every surface is Medium.

checks.title: Launch-page SEO checklist: exactly one `<h1>`, `<title>` under 60 characters, meta description under 160, canonical URL, robots.txt, sitemap.xml, complete OG and Twitter tags, JSON-LD, HTTPS-only references, and no leftover template noindex. CWV code signals cross-reference F-SEO per the ownership map
checks.look: landing head markup, `public/robots.txt`, sitemap config for the marketing surface; grep `noindex` in shipped HTML.
checks.fail: a leftover noindex on the shipped landing surface is Critical (silent de-indexing); missing OG or Twitter tags is Medium; an over-length title or description is Low.

checks.title: OG card to spec: exactly 1200x630, under 300KB, product name plus a 6-10 word value prop legible at 600x315, brand color present, nothing critical in the outer 40px
checks.look: `public/og*.png`, `opengraph-image.*` route output; image metadata via `identify` or `sips -g pixelWidth -g pixelHeight`.
checks.fail: wrong dimensions or over 300KB is Medium; a logo-only card with no product name or value prop is Medium.

checks.title: Five-channel preview evidence: the OG card was previewed on X, LinkedIn (Post Inspector), Slack, iMessage, and Discord before any link shipped, with screenshots or a checked runbook row at or before D-1 as evidence
checks.look: `.launch-ready/assets/og-previews/`, runbook rows, the launch log.
checks.fail: no preview evidence with a launch date set or passed is Medium (LinkedIn caches a wrong card for 7 days); a preview row scheduled after link-sharing tasks is Low.

checks.title: The waitlist is not paper: a capture form of two fields max, double opt-in where the confirmation email precedes list entry, an inline thank-you naming the confirmation email, and a welcome email within 5 minutes of confirmation
checks.look: form markup, `api/subscribe*` handlers, ESP config and send call sites.
checks.fail: capture with no double opt-in is High (paper waitlist); no welcome send path is Medium; a form over two fields is Low.

checks.title: The email sequence is complete: 2-4 pre-launch emails each with a stated purpose, a launch-day drop, and D+1, D+3, D+7 post-launch emails drafted or templated. Transactional email (resets, receipts) belongs to product surfaces, not this funnel
checks.look: `docs/launch/emails/`, ESP exports, email template directories.
checks.fail: no post-launch emails planned is Medium (silent fade); purpose-less drip filler is Low.

checks.title: Sending-domain authentication and consent: SPF, DKIM, and DMARC recorded in IaC or documented for the sending domain, a list-level unsubscribe in every template, a GDPR consent checkbox when EU or UK users are in scope
checks.look: DNS IaC and zone files, `docs/launch/dns-email-auth.md`, email footers, form consent markup.
checks.fail: scheduled sends with no DMARC evidence is High (a launch-day drop from an unauthenticated domain lands in spam with no retry); a mailto-the-founder unsubscribe is Medium.

checks.title: Channel plans follow venue etiquette: each in-scope channel has all seven fields (venue, timing with timezone, title, body, hunter or submitter, amplification plan, response plan); the Show HN title has no "launch" and no all-caps, the PH slot is 12:01 AM PT Tue-Thu with a hunter confirmed more than 48 hours out, Reddit targets have real prior participation at 9:1, LinkedIn is founder voice
checks.look: `docs/launch/channels.md`, post drafts in launch assets.
checks.fail: an etiquette-violating title or slot in a committed plan is Medium; channel drafts carrying under half the seven fields is Medium.

checks.title: UTM discipline: a utm-registry exists and every shareable link in launch assets (emails, post drafts, press kit) carries registered `utm_source`, `utm_medium`, `utm_campaign`, and `utm_content` values
checks.look: `docs/launch/utm-registry.md`, grep `utm_` across email templates and channel drafts, grep bare product URLs in the same files.
checks.fail: shared links with no UTM params is High (silent launch; arrived traffic can never be retro-tagged); registry value collisions is Medium.

checks.title: The conversion waterfall is instrumented: the five events (visit, CTA click, form submit, confirmation, activation) have call sites in code and a staging test that fires and verifies each one
checks.look: analytics calls (`plausible(`, `posthog.capture`, `gtag(`) around the CTA, form, confirmation, and activation paths; `tests/launch/`.
checks.fail: analytics installed with no waterfall events is High (attribution blind); no staging test at funded-product scale is Medium.

checks.title: Amplification and response are named: at least 5 named humans or named roles with specific asks sent more than 48 hours ahead, and the founder blocked as primary responder for the 8-hour window after each post
checks.look: the channels doc's amplification and response sections, runbook rows.
checks.fail: no amplification list with broad channels in scope is Medium; a delegated-only response plan is Low.

checks.title: The runbook is real: D-7 to D+7 as a timezone-aware calendar, every row with a date, owner, and pass criterion, an hour-by-hour launch-day schedule, and a retrospective row with numeric targets set before launch
checks.look: `docs/launch/RUNBOOK.md`, `.launch-ready/launches/`.
checks.fail: a set launch date with no runbook is Medium; rows without owners or pass criteria is Low; a retrospective without pre-set targets is Low.

checks.title: Sibling coupling holds: the status page is hosted off the app's infrastructure and linked from the footer, the launch date does not land atop an in-progress expand/contract migration, and at-risk SLOs get an SLO-watch runbook row. Migration mechanics and SLO definitions stay with deploy and observe; audit only the coupling and cross-reference F-DEPLOY and F-OBS ids per the ownership map
checks.look: footer links, status provider config and its hosting, the deploy migration schedule, SLO docs.
checks.fail: a same-infra status page is High (discovered useless during the first outage, often launch day); a launch date mid-migration is High.

checks.title: The cold proof checkpoint exists: the runbook ends with the cold proof test naming all five observations (a stranger describes the product in under 15 seconds, the CTA reaches a working waitlist, confirmation arrives within 5 minutes, the OG preview renders correctly from any of the five channels, the visit lands in analytics under the right UTM source)
checks.look: the runbook's final checkpoint row, launch log entries.
checks.fail: no cold proof checkpoint on a full-launch (tier 4) plan is Medium; a checkpoint missing observations is Low.

checks.title: No fabricated or dead social surfaces: no invented testimonials, no logos of non-customers, no "as seen in" badges for outlets that never covered the product, no placeholder user counts, no lorem ipsum, no dead "Learn more" links
checks.look: social-proof markup, testimonial data files, anchor hrefs on the landing page, grep `lorem`.
checks.fail: fabricated proof is High (trust and legal exposure on the most public surface); lorem ipsum or dead links on a shipped landing is Medium.

checks.title: Launch state matches reality: state and registry files agree with the repo and the calendar; a passed launch date with status still pre-launch, a UTM campaign slug reused across launches, or a completed launch with no retrospective are drift
checks.look: `.launch-ready/STATE.md` dates against today, utm-registry campaign values, `retrospectives/` against the launch log.
checks.fail: reused campaign slugs polluting analytics history is Medium; stale state contradicting a completed launch is Low.
